openingMessage: You can't control what happens on a location. You can control whether your team is ready before they get there.
leadershipThought: The people who set their crew up to succeed are the ones who thought about failure first. Prep is not pessimism — it is respect for the job.
opsContent:
Thursday Active Status (2026-06-26):
• Alex Clubb — Christi (vessel work): Was returning under air to close manway and tackle sludge/gases. Confirm current clearance status before any crew approaches the vessel area. Supplied air and monitoring remain required until explicitly cleared.
• Michael Hutt — SM Coonskin pad: Job extended through June 29. Elevated H2S carry-forward. Monitoring plan must be confirmed before any site visit.
• Ralph Forbes — University 6-48A #101H: Awaiting Exxon job status confirmation. Do not mobilize until update received.
• Dennis Easley / Larry Taylor — SM Energy Gipple: Gipple 134 rig maintenance ongoing. Gipple 124H H2S run ongoing. Both require current escalation and monitoring plan before crew is on-site.

Discussion item this morning:
• Trailer field readiness — see alert on this page. A trailer went to the field missing SCBA units (x4), muster stands (x2), entrance signage, and fire extinguisher. This is the discussion. Who owns the load-out check, and what does the standard look like before a trailer leaves the yard.
exxonMoments:
Three Jobs, Same Standard — Nathan Contreras (Operational Discipline)
King D #4901 at 8:56 AM — full protocol on the first job of the day: atmospheric testing, LOTO, exclusion zones, respiratory protection, life saving rules discussed, crew field-verified conditions rather than trusting paperwork. Left safe. He then ran the same standard at Tracy B #1 and Donovan 2U 121. Three jobs, one day, zero shortcuts. Nathan Contreras, 06/22.

Unsafe Conditions Managed Early — University 3-19 705H (Operational Discipline)
Unsafe conditions flagged at arrival. All LOTO verified correct before work proceeded. Crew communicated with PIC and between themselves throughout. Field conditions verified — not taken on faith from paperwork. Location left better than found, tools and equipment picked up. Raymundo Solis, 06/22 — first job of his day, before the University 702H heat lesson later that afternoon.

MT Brought Everyone In — Helwig 3 TM (Operational Discipline)
Rod well reconnect completed safely in Big Lake. Standout note: the MT did an exceptional job involving all personnel on-site in the pretask briefing — not just the Canon tech, every person working the job. That is the difference between a check-box pretask and a pretask that actually prepares the crew. Caleb Smith, 06/22.

Crew-Wide Discipline — Oliver 39 (Atmospheric Monitoring)
Full control package confirmed: LOTO, exclusion zones, atmospheric monitoring, field-verified conditions. Strong communication with both PIC and crew throughout. Job completed and left safe. Andrew Bailey ran the same protocol as Justin Fontenot on the same location — showing the standard held across the whole crew, not just one tech. 06/22.

fieldNews.headline: Alex Clubb — Rescue Team in Place Before the Break
fieldNews.detail: Before breaking containment on the choke job, Alex held a full safety meeting with the Superintendent, PO, and PSMS on site. One person under air for the break, himself as primary rescue, Justin as secondary. After the break was made, he pulled the crew back, entered with Justin as rescuer, and conducted AGT — 0s on T for all well headers. Only then did the crew proceed without air. That is confined space rescue protocol executed out loud, in sequence, with no shortcuts. June 17.

fieldNews.headline: Brianda Sandoval — Gipple Done Clean
fieldNews.detail: Finished at Gipple, swapped equipment, handed off the location, and moved to the next stop without a gap. Dennis Easley posted 'Thank you Bri' publicly in the channel. That kind of acknowledgment in front of the whole crew means something — it got earned.

fieldNews.headline: Marylou Guerra — Back-to-Back Battery Tanks
fieldNews.detail: Finished water battery tank 1 and immediately communicated the next step: prepping and cleaning oil battery tank 1. No dead air, no wait for direction. Sequential job execution with clear status at every transition. That is how multi-step jobs stay on schedule. June 17.

fieldNews.headline: Jacob Hernandez — Full Day of Communication
fieldNews.detail: Three location updates across the day — SDU job start, SDU 1602A complete and moving, SDU 1943A arrival and job started. First message before 8 AM, last after 5:30 PM. Consistent updates all day so leadership always knew where he was and what was happening. June 17.

fieldAlert.title: Trailer Went to the Field — Not Field Ready
fieldAlert.body: A trailer was dispatched to a job site this week that was not in field-ready condition. Four SCBA units were absent, two muster stands were missing, entrance signage was not in place, and the fire extinguisher was not on the unit. These are not optional items — they are the foundation of what we promise every crew member who steps on a location. The trailer is not a vehicle. It is a command post, a refuge point, and the first line of response if something goes wrong. When a muster stand is missing, there is no designated rally point in an emergency. When an SCBA is absent, a tech cannot re-enter a hot zone to pull someone out. When the fire extinguisher is gone and nobody notices, we have already accepted a condition we would never accept on the wellhead. Field readiness is not an inventory check — it is a standard of care for the people doing the work. If a trailer leaves the yard short, that is a system failure. Dispatchers, techs loading out, and supervisors signing off all have a role in catching it before it moves. Tomorrow we are going to talk about what the standard looks like, who owns it, and how we close the gap.

todaysChallenge: Close every open loop before you leave today. If it is not done — say so. If it carries to Monday — hand it off clearly.
todaysChallengeSubtext: End-of-week ambiguity is the start of next-week incidents. Be the one who closes it clean.

compliance.footerNote: Update numbers in daily.json each Monday morning.
